rawText:
Even as solar panels and wind turbines claim ever-larger shares of the world's electricity mix, a stubborn arithmetic haunts the energy transition: the final fraction of power that renewables cannot reliably supply gives natural gas an outsized hold on energy security. A grid that draws ninety percent of its annual electricity from clean sources does not run on a steady nine-to-one blend. Instead, it operates almost entirely on renewables most of the time — and then, for roughly a tenth of all hours, depends almost entirely on gas. Those hours, when the wind dies and clouds linger and batteries drain, are the moments that define whether a country keeps its lights on.

This structural dependency explains why the current disruption to Gulf shipping lanes has sent power prices surging across continents even in countries that have invested heavily in renewables. Spain, which has built one of Europe's most aggressive solar and wind portfolios, still relies on gas to set power prices fifteen percent of the time — a figure that rises sharply during winter evenings and prolonged calm weather. The International Energy Agency noted strong gas-price volatility across Asia, Europe, and North America in January 2026, driven by adverse weather and geopolitical tensions. Countries that assumed their growing renewable capacity would insulate them from fossil-fuel shocks are discovering that dependency does not decline in proportion to average consumption.

The technologies that could eventually fill this gap remain years from commercial scale. Iron-air batteries, which promise storage lasting days rather than hours at a fraction of lithium-ion costs, have begun their first utility-scale deployments in the United States, with one manufacturer targeting an installed cost below twenty dollars per kilowatt-hour. Hydrogen storage and advanced flow batteries offer theoretical solutions for seasonal imbalances, but the global market for long-duration storage reached just $3.6 billion in 2025 — a fraction of what would be required to displace gas as the grid's backstop. New nuclear plants remain prohibitively expensive, and hydroelectric capacity depends on geography and rainfall that climate change is making less predictable.

The paradox deepens as gas markets themselves evolve. The IEA projects a sixty-five-billion-cubic-meter surplus of liquefied natural gas by 2030 as new export terminals flood the market, yet much of that supply is concentrated in North America, which accounted for over eighty-five percent of global LNG supply growth in 2026. Several emerging economies in Asia are bypassing gas infrastructure altogether, moving directly from coal to solar. If global gas demand falls while supply concentrates among fewer producers, the market could become thinner and more prone to the kind of acute shortages that the current crisis exemplifies. Gas may power a smaller share of the world's electricity in the future, but the hours it covers will remain the most consequential — and the most expensive.

sentences.original: Even as solar panels and wind turbines claim ever-larger shares of the world's electricity mix, a stubborn arithmetic haunts the energy transition: the final fraction of power that renewables cannot reliably supply gives natural gas an outsized hold on energy security.
sentences.translation: 태양광 패널과 풍력 터빈이 세계 전력 구성에서 점점 더 큰 비중을 차지하고 있음에도, 완고한 산술이 에너지 전환을 괴롭힌다: 재생에너지가 안정적으로 공급할 수 없는 전력의 마지막 부분이 천연가스에게 에너지 안보에 대한 과도한 지배력을 부여한다.

grammarElements.text: Even as solar panels and wind turbines claim ever-larger shares of the world's electricity mix
grammarElements.label: 양보 부사절 (Even as)

grammarElements.text: the final fraction of power that renewables cannot reliably supply gives natural gas an outsized hold on energy security
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 설명절 (산술의 내용)

vocabulary.example: The stubborn inflation refused to respond to conventional monetary policy.
A handful of tech companies now exert an outsized influence on the global economy.
The government pledged to generate half of its electricity from renewables by 2035.

expressions.usage: 피할 수 없는 수학적 현실이 계획이나 정책을 방해할 때, 추상 개념을 의인화하여 사용하는 수사적 표현
비중에 비해 불균형적으로 큰 영향력을 행사하는 상황을 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.explanation: 긍정적 추세에도 불구하고 해소되지 않는 구조적 문제를 콜론으로 설명하는 도입 패턴
examples: Even as vaccination rates climb across the region, a stubborn variant threatens the recovery: the virus mutates faster than health systems can adapt.
Even as digital payments spread to rural areas, a fundamental gap persists: the infrastructure needed for reliable connectivity remains absent.

drillQuestions.question: Even as solar panels and wind turbines claim ever-larger shares, a _______ arithmetic haunts the energy transition.

sentences.notes: 기사의 첫 문장이자 핵심 논제. 'stubborn arithmetic'이라는 의인화 표현이 에너지 전환의 구조적 한계를 수학적 필연으로 프레이밍한다. 콜론 뒤에서 그 산술의 구체적 내용을 풀어낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: Even as solar panels and wind turbines claim ever-larger shares
koreanAlignment.ko: 태양광 패널과 풍력 터빈이 점점 더 큰 비중을 차지하고 있음에도

koreanAlignment.en: a stubborn arithmetic haunts the energy transition
koreanAlignment.ko: 완고한 산술이 에너지 전환을 괴롭힌다

koreanAlignment.en: gives natural gas an outsized hold on energy security
koreanAlignment.ko: 천연가스에게 에너지 안보에 대한 과도한 지배력을 부여한다

sentences.original: A grid that draws ninety percent of its annual electricity from clean sources does not run on a steady nine-to-one blend.
sentences.translation: 연간 전력의 90퍼센트를 청정 에너지원에서 끌어오는 전력망이라 해도 안정적인 9대 1 비율로 운영되는 것은 아니다.

grammarElements.text: A grid that draws ninety percent of its annual electricity from clean sources
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 관계절 수식

vocabulary.example: The national grid struggled to meet demand during the heatwave.
The country's energy blend has shifted dramatically toward solar in the past decade.

expressions.usage: 구성 비율을 직관적으로 전달할 때 사용하는 표현으로, 실제 운영이 평균과 다름을 강조하기 위한 비유

patterns.pattern: [A system] that [achieves X percent] does not [operate as expected]
patterns.explanation: 평균적 수치가 실제 운영 방식을 오도한다는 점을 지적하는 패턴
examples: A school that graduates ninety-five percent of its students does not necessarily educate them equally well.
A hospital that treats a thousand patients daily does not deliver the same quality of care to each one.

drillQuestions.question: A grid that draws ninety percent of its annual electricity from clean sources does not run on a steady nine-to-one _______.

sentences.notes: 평균의 함정을 지적하는 핵심 문장. 90퍼센트라는 수치가 안정적 9:1 운영을 의미하지 않는다는 반직관적 사실을 제시하여 다음 문장의 설명을 준비한다.

koreanAlignment.en: A grid that draws ninety percent of its annual electricity
koreanAlignment.ko: 연간 전력의 90퍼센트를 끌어오는 전력망

koreanAlignment.en: does not run on a steady nine-to-one blend
koreanAlignment.ko: 안정적인 9대 1 비율로 운영되지 않는다

sentences.original: Instead, it operates almost entirely on renewables most of the time — and then, for roughly a tenth of all hours, depends almost entirely on gas.
sentences.translation: 대신, 대부분의 시간에는 거의 전적으로 재생에너지로 운영되다가 — 전체 시간의 대략 10분의 1 동안은 거의 전적으로 가스에 의존한다.

grammarElements.text: almost entirely on renewables most of the time
grammarElements.label: 부사구 (첫 번째 상황)

grammarElements.text: for roughly a tenth of all hours ... almost entirely on gas
grammarElements.label: 부사구 (두 번째 상황)

vocabulary.example: The factory operates around the clock to meet seasonal demand.
Roughly half of the surveyed companies reported declining revenues.

expressions.usage: 의존도가 극단적으로 높은 상황을 묘사할 때 사용. 이 문장에서는 양극단을 대비하기 위해 같은 표현을 두 번 사용

patterns.pattern: Instead, [subject] [does X] most of the time — and then, for [fraction], [does Y]
patterns.explanation: 앞 문장의 부정에 이어 실제 작동 방식을 대시로 구분된 두 가지 상황으로 설명하는 패턴
examples: Instead, the market runs smoothly most of the time — and then, for a few critical days, behaves erratically.
Instead, the algorithm performs accurately most of the time — and then, for edge cases, fails catastrophically.

drillQuestions.question: Instead, it operates almost entirely on renewables most of the time — and then, for roughly a _______ of all hours, depends almost entirely on gas.

sentences.notes: 'almost entirely'를 두 번 반복하되 대상을 renewables에서 gas로 전환함으로써, 에너지 공급의 양극적 성격을 극적으로 드러낸다. 대시가 시간적 전환을 구분한다.

koreanAlignment.en: it operates almost entirely on renewables most of the time
koreanAlignment.ko: 대부분의 시간에는 거의 전적으로 재생에너지로 운영된다

koreanAlignment.en: for roughly a tenth of all hours
koreanAlignment.ko: 전체 시간의 대략 10분의 1 동안

koreanAlignment.en: depends almost entirely on gas
koreanAlignment.ko: 거의 전적으로 가스에 의존한다

sentences.original: Those hours, when the wind dies and clouds linger and batteries drain, are the moments that define whether a country keeps its lights on.
sentences.translation: 바람이 멈추고 구름이 머물고 배터리가 소진되는 그 시간들이야말로 한 나라가 불을 켤 수 있는지를 결정짓는 순간이다.

grammarElements.text: when the wind dies and clouds linger and batteries drain
grammarElements.label: 관계부사절 (시간 설명)

grammarElements.text: the moments that define whether a country keeps its lights on
grammarElements.label: 보어 + 관계절 + whether 간접의문절

vocabulary.example: The fog lingered over the valley well into the afternoon.
Prolonged negotiations drained both sides of their patience and resources.

expressions.usage: 전력 공급의 지속 여부를 직관적 이미지로 표현할 때 사용하는 관용 표현

patterns.pattern: Those [time periods], when [A] and [B] and [C], are the moments that define [outcome]
patterns.explanation: 세 가지 조건을 and로 나열한 뒤 그 시간이 결정적임을 강조하는 수사적 패턴
examples: Those weeks, when supply chains freeze and warehouses empty and orders pile up, are the moments that define whether a retailer survives the season.
Those nights, when servers crash and backups fail and engineers scramble, are the moments that define a platform's reliability.

drillQuestions.question: Those hours, when the wind dies and clouds _______ and batteries drain, are the moments that define whether a country keeps its lights on.

sentences.notes: 첫 단락의 마무리 문장. 'the wind dies / clouds linger / batteries drain'이라는 세 겹의 병렬 구조가 자연의 변덕을 리듬감 있게 전달하며, 'keeps its lights on'이라는 일상적 이미지로 에너지 안보의 본질을 환기한다.

koreanAlignment.en: when the wind dies and clouds linger and batteries drain
koreanAlignment.ko: 바람이 멈추고 구름이 머물고 배터리가 소진되는

koreanAlignment.en: the moments that define
koreanAlignment.ko: 결정짓는 순간

koreanAlignment.en: whether a country keeps its lights on
koreanAlignment.ko: 한 나라가 불을 켤 수 있는지

pronunciationNotes: linger는 '링거'로 g를 살려 발음 (ng 뒤에 g 추가)

sentences.original: This structural dependency explains why the current disruption to Gulf shipping lanes has sent power prices surging across continents even in countries that have invested heavily in renewables.
sentences.translation: 이 구조적 의존성은 현재 걸프 해운 항로의 혼란이 재생에너지에 막대한 투자를 한 국가들에서조차 대륙 전체에 걸쳐 전력 가격을 급등시킨 이유를 설명한다.

grammarElements.text: why the current disruption to Gulf shipping lanes has sent power prices surging across continents even in countries that have invested heavily in renewables
grammarElements.label: why 간접의문절 (목적어)

vocabulary.example: The cyberattack caused major disruption to the banking system for three days.
Surging demand for semiconductors has created a global shortage.

expressions.usage: 일시적이 아닌 체계에 내재된 근본적 의존 관계를 지칭할 때 사용하는 분석적 표현

patterns.pattern: This [structural feature] explains why [event] has [impact] even in [seemingly protected group]
patterns.explanation: 구조적 원인이 예상 밖의 대상에까지 영향을 미치는 이유를 설명하는 인과 패턴
examples: This fiscal imbalance explains why the interest rate hike has squeezed budgets even in countries with strong reserves.
This architectural flaw explains why the software update has caused crashes even in devices running the latest hardware.

drillQuestions.question: This structural _______ explains why the current disruption to Gulf shipping lanes has sent power prices surging.

sentences.notes: 두 번째 단락의 주제문으로, 'This structural dependency'가 첫 단락의 논점을 실제 사례로 연결하는 전환 장치 역할을 한다. 'even in countries that have invested heavily'가 아이러니를 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: This structural dependency explains why
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 구조적 의존성은 ~인 이유를 설명한다

koreanAlignment.en: the current disruption to Gulf shipping lanes
koreanAlignment.ko: 현재 걸프 해운 항로의 혼란

koreanAlignment.en: even in countries that have invested heavily in renewables
koreanAlignment.ko: 재생에너지에 막대한 투자를 한 국가들에서조차

sentences.original: Spain, which has built one of Europe's most aggressive solar and wind portfolios, still relies on gas to set power prices fifteen percent of the time — a figure that rises sharply during winter evenings and prolonged calm weather.
sentences.translation: 유럽에서 가장 공격적인 태양광 및 풍력 포트폴리오를 구축한 스페인조차 여전히 전력 가격을 설정하는 시간의 15퍼센트를 가스에 의존한다 — 이 수치는 겨울 저녁과 장기간의 무풍 날씨 동안 급격히 상승한다.

grammarElements.text: which has built one of Europe's most aggressive solar and wind portfolios
grammarElements.label: 비제한적 관계절 (배경 정보)

grammarElements.text: gas to set power prices fifteen percent of the time
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + to 부정사 목적

grammarElements.text: a figure that rises sharply during winter evenings and prolonged calm weather
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 동격 명사구 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: The company pursued an aggressive expansion strategy across Southeast Asia.
The pension fund diversified its portfolio to include emerging market bonds.
A prolonged drought forced farmers to abandon their fields.

expressions.phrase: relies on gas to set power prices
expressions.meaning: 전력 가격을 설정하는 데 가스에 의존하다
expressions.usage: 한계 가격 설정자(marginal price setter)로서 가스의 역할을 설명할 때 사용하는 에너지 시장 표현

patterns.pattern: [Country], which has [impressive achievement], still [depends on X] — a figure that [worsens under condition]
patterns.explanation: 인상적 성과에도 불구하고 여전히 존재하는 의존성을 대시 뒤에서 조건부로 악화시키는 패턴
examples: Germany, which has installed more wind capacity than any other European nation, still imports electricity during peak demand — a pattern that intensifies in windless January weeks.
Japan, which has achieved remarkable energy efficiency, still relies on imported LNG for thirty percent of its power — a share that climbs during summer air-conditioning peaks.

drillQuestions.question: Spain still relies on gas to set power prices fifteen percent of the time — a figure that rises sharply during winter evenings and _______ calm weather.

sentences.notes: 스페인이라는 구체적 사례가 앞선 이론적 논점을 실증한다. 비제한적 관계절이 스페인의 재생에너지 투자를 먼저 인정한 뒤, 'still'이 역설을 도입하고, 대시 뒤에서 상황의 가변성을 추가로 드러낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: one of Europe's most aggressive solar and wind portfolios
koreanAlignment.ko: 유럽에서 가장 공격적인 태양광 및 풍력 포트폴리오

koreanAlignment.en: still relies on gas to set power prices
koreanAlignment.ko: 여전히 전력 가격 설정을 가스에 의존한다

koreanAlignment.en: prolonged calm weather
koreanAlignment.ko: 장기간의 무풍 날씨

sentences.original: The International Energy Agency noted strong gas-price volatility across Asia, Europe, and North America in January 2026, driven by adverse weather and geopolitical tensions.
sentences.translation: 국제에너지기구(IEA)는 악천후와 지정학적 긴장에 의해 촉발되어 2026년 1월 아시아, 유럽, 북미 전역에서 강한 가스 가격 변동성이 나타났다고 지적했다.

grammarElements.text: strong gas-price volatility across Asia, Europe, and North America in January 2026
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + 장소/시간 부사구

grammarElements.text: driven by adverse weather and geopolitical tensions
grammarElements.label: 과거분사구 (원인)

vocabulary.example: Currency volatility makes it difficult for exporters to plan their pricing strategies.
Adverse market conditions forced several startups to delay their IPOs.
Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have historically affected oil prices.

expressions.phrase: driven by adverse weather and geopolitical tensions
expressions.usage: 복합적 원인을 간결하게 과거분사구로 부기할 때 사용하는 보도 표현

patterns.explanation: 권위 있는 기관의 관찰을 인용한 뒤 과거분사구로 원인을 덧붙이는 경제 보도 패턴
examples: The World Bank noted rising food insecurity across sub-Saharan Africa, driven by drought and supply chain disruptions.
The Federal Reserve noted persistent wage pressures across the service sector, driven by labor shortages and immigration restrictions.

drillQuestions.question: The International Energy Agency noted strong gas-price _______ across Asia, Europe, and North America.

sentences.notes: IEA라는 권위 있는 기관의 관찰을 인용하여 논증에 신뢰성을 부여한다. 'driven by' 분사구가 원인을 경제적으로 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The International Energy Agency noted
koreanAlignment.ko: 국제에너지기구가 지적했다

koreanAlignment.en: strong gas-price volatility across Asia, Europe, and North America
koreanAlignment.ko: 아시아, 유럽, 북미 전역의 강한 가스 가격 변동성

koreanAlignment.en: driven by adverse weather and geopolitical tensions
koreanAlignment.ko: 악천후와 지정학적 긴장에 의해 촉발된

sentences.original: Countries that assumed their growing renewable capacity would insulate them from fossil-fuel shocks are discovering that dependency does not decline in proportion to average consumption.
sentences.translation: 증가하는 재생에너지 용량이 화석연료 충격으로부터 자국을 단열시켜줄 것이라고 가정했던 국가들은 의존도가 평균 소비에 비례하여 감소하지 않는다는 사실을 발견하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: Countries that assumed their growing renewable capacity would insulate them from fossil-fuel shocks
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 관계절 (과거 가정)

grammarElements.text: that dependency does not decline in proportion to average consumption
grammarElements.label: that 명사절 (목적어)

vocabulary.example: Diversified supply chains are supposed to insulate companies from regional disruptions.
Costs did not fall in proportion to the decline in output.

expressions.usage: 이전의 가정이 현실에 의해 반박되고 있음을 실시간으로 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Actors] that assumed [X] would [protect them] are discovering that [X does not work as expected]
patterns.explanation: 과거의 잘못된 가정과 현재의 깨달음을 대비시키는 분석 패턴
examples: Investors that assumed diversification would shield them from losses are discovering that correlated markets undermine the strategy.
Governments that assumed automation would create as many jobs as it destroyed are discovering that displacement outpaces retraining.

drillQuestions.question: Countries that assumed their growing renewable capacity would _______ them from fossil-fuel shocks are discovering that dependency does not decline in proportion to average consumption.

sentences.notes: 두 번째 단락의 마무리로, 기사 전체의 핵심 논점을 일반화한다. 'assumed'(과거 가정)과 'are discovering'(현재진행형 깨달음)의 시제 대비가 인식 전환의 과정을 포착한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Countries that assumed their growing renewable capacity would insulate them
koreanAlignment.ko: 재생에너지 용량이 자국을 보호해줄 것이라고 가정했던 국가들

koreanAlignment.en: are discovering that
koreanAlignment.ko: ~라는 사실을 발견하고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: dependency does not decline in proportion to average consumption
koreanAlignment.ko: 의존도가 평균 소비에 비례하여 감소하지 않는다

sentences.original: The technologies that could eventually fill this gap remain years from commercial scale.
sentences.translation: 이 격차를 궁극적으로 메울 수 있는 기술들은 상용화까지 아직 수년이 남아 있다.

grammarElements.text: The technologies that could eventually fill this gap
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 관계절 수식

vocabulary.example: The technology has been proven in laboratories but has not yet reached commercial scale.

expressions.usage: 기술이나 정책이 실현까지 상당한 시간이 필요함을 간결하게 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: The [solutions] that could [solve problem] remain [time] from [milestone]
patterns.explanation: 잠재적 해결책이 아직 실현 단계에 이르지 못했음을 강조하는 전환 패턴
examples: The vaccines that could eventually eradicate the disease remain a decade from widespread distribution.
The reforms that could stabilize the pension system remain years from legislative approval.

drillQuestions.question: The technologies that could eventually fill this gap remain years from _______ scale.

sentences.notes: 세 번째 단락의 주제문으로, 짧고 단정적인 구조가 기술적 해결책의 부재를 단호하게 선언한다. 'remain years from'이 시간적 거리를 물리적 거리처럼 표현한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The technologies that could eventually fill this gap
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 격차를 궁극적으로 메울 수 있는 기술들

koreanAlignment.en: remain years from commercial scale
koreanAlignment.ko: 상용화까지 아직 수년이 남아 있다

sentences.original: Iron-air batteries, which promise storage lasting days rather than hours at a fraction of lithium-ion costs, have begun their first utility-scale deployments in the United States, with one manufacturer targeting an installed cost below twenty dollars per kilowatt-hour.
sentences.translation: 리튬이온 비용의 일부분으로 시간이 아닌 며칠간의 저장을 약속하는 철-공기 배터리는 미국에서 첫 유틸리티 규모 배치를 시작했으며, 한 제조업체는 킬로와트시당 20달러 이하의 설치 비용을 목표로 하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: which promise storage lasting days rather than hours at a fraction of lithium-ion costs
grammarElements.label: 비제한적 관계절 (기술 설명)

grammarElements.text: their first utility-scale deployments in the United States
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: with one manufacturer targeting an installed cost below twenty dollars per kilowatt-hour
grammarElements.label: with + 독립분사구문 (부대상황)

vocabulary.example: The rapid deployment of 5G networks has transformed mobile connectivity in urban areas.
The new material can be produced at a fraction of the cost of traditional alternatives.

expressions.phrase: at a fraction of lithium-ion costs
expressions.usage: 기존 기술 대비 혁신적으로 낮은 비용을 강조할 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Technology], which promise [advantage] at a fraction of [existing] costs, have begun [deployment], with [detail]
patterns.explanation: 신기술의 이점을 관계절로 소개한 뒤 배치 현황을 기술하고 with 구문으로 세부 정보를 추가하는 기술 보도 패턴
examples: Solid-state batteries, which promise faster charging at a fraction of current safety risks, have begun their first automotive applications, with one manufacturer targeting mass production by 2028.

Modular reactors, which promise nuclear power at a fraction of traditional construction costs, have begun their first licensing reviews, with one developer targeting commercial operation within five years.

drillQuestions.question: Iron-air batteries have begun their first utility-scale _______ in the United States.

sentences.notes: 기술적 상세 정보가 밀집된 문장. 비제한적 관계절이 철-공기 배터리의 핵심 장점을 압축하고, with 분사구문이 구체적 비용 목표를 추가한다. 정보 밀도가 높지만 구조는 논리적으로 정돈되어 있다.

koreanAlignment.en: Iron-air batteries, which promise storage lasting days rather than hours
koreanAlignment.ko: 시간이 아닌 며칠간의 저장을 약속하는 철-공기 배터리

koreanAlignment.en: at a fraction of lithium-ion costs
koreanAlignment.ko: 리튬이온 비용의 일부분으로

koreanAlignment.en: with one manufacturer targeting an installed cost below twenty dollars per kilowatt-hour
koreanAlignment.ko: 한 제조업체가 킬로와트시당 20달러 이하의 설치 비용을 목표로 하고 있으며

sentences.original: Hydrogen storage and advanced flow batteries offer theoretical solutions for seasonal imbalances, but the global market for long-duration storage reached just $3.6 billion in 2025 — a fraction of what would be required to displace gas as the grid's backstop.
sentences.translation: 수소 저장과 첨단 플로우 배터리는 계절적 불균형에 대한 이론적 해결책을 제시하지만, 장기 저장 글로벌 시장은 2025년에 겨우 36억 달러에 도달했을 뿐이다 — 가스를 전력망의 안전장치로서 대체하기 위해 필요한 것의 일부에 불과하다.

grammarElements.text: Hydrogen storage and advanced flow batteries
grammarElements.label: 주어 (병렬)

grammarElements.text: but the global market for long-duration storage reached just $3.6 billion in 2025
grammarElements.label: 역접 등위절

grammarElements.text: a fraction of what would be required to displace gas as the grid's backstop
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 동격 명사구 (규모 평가)

vocabulary.example: The central bank serves as the financial system's backstop during liquidity crises.
Electric vehicles are expected to displace internal combustion engines over the next two decades.
The trade imbalance between the two nations has widened for the third consecutive year.

expressions.usage: '이론적'이라는 수식어가 실현 가능성에 대한 회의적 거리두기를 내포하는 표현
전력 시스템에서 최후의 신뢰 가능한 공급원을 은유적으로 지칭할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [X] offer theoretical solutions for [problem], but [market/reality metric] reached just [small number] — a fraction of what would be required to [goal]
patterns.explanation: 이론적 가능성과 현실적 규모의 격차를 but 절과 대시로 이중 강조하는 패턴
examples: Carbon capture technologies offer theoretical solutions for industrial emissions, but global investment reached just twelve billion dollars in 2025 — a fraction of what would be required to meet net-zero targets.

Desalination plants offer theoretical solutions for water scarcity, but installed capacity reached just one percent of global freshwater use — a fraction of what would be required to serve drought-prone regions.

drillQuestions.question: The global market for long-duration storage reached just $3.6 billion — a fraction of what would be required to _______ gas as the grid's backstop.

sentences.notes: 이론과 현실의 격차를 but과 대시라는 이중 장치로 표현한다. 'theoretical'이 이미 회의적 거리를 내포하고, '$3.6 billion'이라는 구체적 수치가 불충분함을 실증하며, 'a fraction of'가 첫 단락의 'fraction' 표현과 반향을 이룬다.

koreanAlignment.en: Hydrogen storage and advanced flow batteries offer theoretical solutions
koreanAlignment.ko: 수소 저장과 첨단 플로우 배터리는 이론적 해결책을 제시하지만

koreanAlignment.en: the global market for long-duration storage reached just $3.6 billion
koreanAlignment.ko: 장기 저장 글로벌 시장은 겨우 36억 달러에 도달했다

koreanAlignment.en: a fraction of what would be required to displace gas as the grid's backstop
koreanAlignment.ko: 전력망의 안전장치로서 가스를 대체하기 위해 필요한 것의 일부

sentences.original: New nuclear plants remain prohibitively expensive, and hydroelectric capacity depends on geography and rainfall that climate change is making less predictable.
sentences.translation: 신규 원자력 발전소는 여전히 감당할 수 없을 만큼 비싸며, 수력 발전 용량은 기후변화가 점점 덜 예측 가능하게 만들고 있는 지리와 강수량에 의존한다.

grammarElements.text: and hydroelectric capacity depends on geography and rainfall that climate change is making less predictable
grammarElements.label: 등위절 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: Housing in central London has become prohibitively expensive for middle-income families.
Norway generates nearly all of its electricity from hydroelectric sources.

expressions.usage: 비용이 너무 높아 실질적으로 실현이 불가능함을 함축할 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Option A] remain prohibitively [adjective], and [Option B] depends on [factors] that [complication]
patterns.explanation: 두 가지 대안을 각각 다른 이유로 기각하며 and로 병렬 연결하는 패턴
examples: New desalination plants remain prohibitively slow to build, and groundwater reserves depend on aquifer levels that overuse is steadily depleting.
Orbital launches remain prohibitively risky for passengers, and alternative propulsion systems depend on materials that supply chains cannot yet reliably deliver.

drillQuestions.question: New nuclear plants remain _______ expensive, and hydroelectric capacity depends on geography and rainfall.

sentences.notes: 두 가지 대안(원자력, 수력)을 하나의 문장에서 각각 다른 이유로 기각한다. 'prohibitively'가 원자력의 비용 장벽을, 'climate change is making less predictable'가 수력의 불확실성을 짧고 효과적으로 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: New nuclear plants remain prohibitively expensive
koreanAlignment.ko: 신규 원자력 발전소는 감당할 수 없을 만큼 비싸다

koreanAlignment.en: hydroelectric capacity depends on geography and rainfall
koreanAlignment.ko: 수력 발전 용량은 지리와 강수량에 의존한다

koreanAlignment.en: that climate change is making less predictable
koreanAlignment.ko: 기후변화가 점점 덜 예측 가능하게 만들고 있는

sentences.original: The paradox deepens as gas markets themselves evolve.
sentences.translation: 가스 시장 자체가 진화하면서 역설은 더욱 깊어진다.

vocabulary.example: The paradox of thrift holds that individual saving can collectively reduce economic growth.
Consumer preferences have evolved rapidly in the age of social media.

expressions.usage: 이미 모순적인 상황이 추가적 요인에 의해 더 복잡해질 때 사용하는 분석적 표현

patterns.explanation: 단락 전환 시 새로운 차원의 복잡성을 도입하는 간결한 주제문 패턴
examples: The dilemma deepens as consumer expectations shift toward instant delivery.
The crisis deepens as neighboring countries impose their own export restrictions.

drillQuestions.question: The _______ deepens as gas markets themselves evolve.

sentences.notes: 네 번째 단락의 주제문. 짧고 강력한 구조로 논의에 새로운 차원을 추가한다. 'themselves'가 가스 시장이 수동적 대상이 아닌 능동적 변화 주체임을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The paradox deepens
koreanAlignment.ko: 역설은 더욱 깊어진다

koreanAlignment.en: as gas markets themselves evolve
koreanAlignment.ko: 가스 시장 자체가 진화하면서

sentences.original: The IEA projects a sixty-five-billion-cubic-meter surplus of liquefied natural gas by 2030 as new export terminals flood the market, yet much of that supply is concentrated in North America, which accounted for over eighty-five percent of global LNG supply growth in 2026.
sentences.translation: IEA는 새로운 수출 터미널이 시장에 쏟아져 나오면서 2030년까지 650억 입방미터의 액화천연가스 잉여분을 전망하지만, 그 공급의 대부분은 2026년 글로벌 LNG 공급 성장의 85퍼센트 이상을 차지한 북미에 집중되어 있다.

grammarElements.text: a sixty-five-billion-cubic-meter surplus of liquefied natural gas by 2030
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: as new export terminals flood the market
grammarElements.label: 이유/시간 부사절

grammarElements.text: yet much of that supply is concentrated in North America, which accounted for over eighty-five percent of global LNG supply growth in 2026
grammarElements.label: 역접 등위절 + 비제한적 관계절

vocabulary.example: The country's trade surplus with its largest partner narrowed for the first time in a decade.
Liquefied natural gas can be transported by tanker to markets that pipelines cannot reach.
Too much of the world's chip manufacturing is concentrated in a single region.

expressions.usage: 대량의 상품이나 자원이 시장에 급격히 유입될 때 사용하는 비유적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Authority] projects [large surplus] by [year] as [supply factor], yet [concentration risk]
patterns.explanation: 공급 증가 전망을 제시한 뒤 yet으로 집중 위험이라는 숨겨진 취약점을 드러내는 패턴
examples: Analysts project a two-million-barrel-per-day surplus of crude by 2028 as shale production accelerates, yet eighty percent of that output is concentrated in three US states.
The FAO projects a record grain surplus by 2027 as genetically modified varieties spread, yet the majority of exportable supply is concentrated in four nations vulnerable to drought.

drillQuestions.question: The IEA projects a sixty-five-billion-cubic-meter _______ of liquefied natural gas by 2030.

sentences.notes: 데이터 밀도가 높은 문장으로, 전망(surplus)과 현실(concentration)을 yet으로 대립시킨다. 숫자들(650억, 85퍼센트)이 공급 과잉과 지리적 집중이라는 이중 위험을 구체화한다.

koreanAlignment.en: a sixty-five-billion-cubic-meter surplus of liquefied natural gas
koreanAlignment.ko: 650억 입방미터의 액화천연가스 잉여분

koreanAlignment.en: new export terminals flood the market
koreanAlignment.ko: 새로운 수출 터미널이 시장에 쏟아져 나온다

koreanAlignment.en: much of that supply is concentrated in North America
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 공급의 대부분은 북미에 집중되어 있다

sentences.original: Several emerging economies in Asia are bypassing gas infrastructure altogether, moving directly from coal to solar.
sentences.translation: 아시아의 여러 신흥 경제국들은 가스 인프라를 완전히 건너뛰고 석탄에서 태양광으로 직접 이행하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: moving directly from coal to solar
grammarElements.label: 현재분사구 (결과/부연)

vocabulary.example: Many African nations are bypassing landline telephone networks and adopting mobile technology directly.
Some analysts believe that physical retail could disappear altogether within a generation.

expressions.phrase: moving directly from coal to solar
expressions.usage: 중간 단계를 생략하는 기술적 도약(leapfrogging)을 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Several [actors] are bypassing [intermediate step] altogether, moving directly from [A] to [C]
patterns.explanation: 중간 단계를 건너뛰는 기술적/경제적 도약을 기술하는 패턴
examples: Several rural communities are bypassing traditional banking altogether, moving directly from cash to mobile payments.
Several developing nations are bypassing desktop computing altogether, moving directly from no connectivity to smartphones.

drillQuestions.question: Several emerging economies in Asia are _______ gas infrastructure altogether, moving directly from coal to solar.

sentences.notes: 기술적 도약(leapfrogging) 현상을 간결하게 포착한다. 'altogether'가 가스 단계를 완전히 생략하는 결단의 단호함을 강조하고, 'from coal to solar'가 극단적 전환을 명료하게 드러낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: Several emerging economies in Asia
koreanAlignment.ko: 아시아의 여러 신흥 경제국들

koreanAlignment.en: are bypassing gas infrastructure altogether
koreanAlignment.ko: 가스 인프라를 완전히 건너뛰고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: moving directly from coal to solar
koreanAlignment.ko: 석탄에서 태양광으로 직접 이행하며

sentences.original: If global gas demand falls while supply concentrates among fewer producers, the market could become thinner and more prone to the kind of acute shortages that the current crisis exemplifies.
sentences.translation: 글로벌 가스 수요가 감소하는 동시에 공급이 더 적은 생산자에게 집중된다면, 시장은 더 얇아지고 현재 위기가 예시하는 종류의 급성 부족에 더 취약해질 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: If global gas demand falls while supply concentrates among fewer producers
grammarElements.label: 조건 부사절 + 시간 부사절 (복합)

grammarElements.text: thinner and more prone to the kind of acute shortages that the current crisis exemplifies
grammarElements.label: 형용사 보어 (병렬) + 관계절

vocabulary.example: Countries with narrow export bases are more prone to commodity price shocks.
The hospital faced an acute shortage of ventilators during the surge.
The company's collapse exemplifies the dangers of excessive leverage.

expressions.usage: 시장 유동성 감소와 위험 증가를 병렬로 묘사하는 금융/에너지 분석 표현

patterns.pattern: If [demand falls] while [supply concentrates], [market] could become [more vulnerable] to [specific risk]
patterns.explanation: 수요와 공급의 비대칭적 변화가 시장 취약성을 높이는 시나리오를 제시하는 조건문 패턴
examples: If buyer interest wanes while inventory accumulates in fewer warehouses, the housing market could become thinner and more prone to sudden price corrections.
If passenger numbers decline while routes concentrate among fewer carriers, the airline market could become more fragile and more prone to service disruptions.

drillQuestions.question: If global gas demand falls while supply concentrates among fewer producers, the market could become thinner and more _______ to acute shortages.

sentences.notes: 복합 조건문이 미래 시나리오를 구축한다. if와 while이 수요 감소와 공급 집중이라는 두 가지 동시적 추세를 엮고, 'thinner and more prone to'가 시장의 이중적 취약화를 예고한다.

koreanAlignment.en: If global gas demand falls while supply concentrates among fewer producers
koreanAlignment.ko: 글로벌 가스 수요가 감소하면서 공급이 더 적은 생산자에게 집중된다면

koreanAlignment.en: the market could become thinner
koreanAlignment.ko: 시장은 더 얇아질 수 있다

koreanAlignment.en: the kind of acute shortages that the current crisis exemplifies
koreanAlignment.ko: 현재 위기가 예시하는 종류의 급성 부족

sentences.original: Gas may power a smaller share of the world's electricity in the future, but the hours it covers will remain the most consequential — and the most expensive.
sentences.translation: 가스는 미래에 세계 전력의 더 작은 비중을 담당하게 될 수 있지만, 가스가 담당하는 시간은 가장 중대한 — 그리고 가장 비싼 — 시간으로 남을 것이다.

grammarElements.text: a smaller share of the world's electricity in the future
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: but the hours it covers will remain the most consequential — and the most expensive
grammarElements.label: 역접 등위절 + 대시 병렬

vocabulary.example: The court's ruling was the most consequential legal decision on climate policy in a decade.

expressions.phrase: the most consequential — and the most expensive
expressions.usage: 대시를 사용해 추가적 특성을 극적으로 덧붙이는 수사적 마무리 기법

patterns.pattern: [X] may [decrease/change] in the future, but [the part that remains] will be the most [adjective] — and the most [adjective]
patterns.explanation: 전체적 감소에도 불구하고 잔여 부분의 중요성이 오히려 증가한다는 역설을 마무리하는 결론 패턴
examples: Human labor may perform a smaller share of total tasks in the future, but the decisions it handles will remain the most complex — and the most consequential.
Print media may reach a smaller audience in the future, but the readers it retains will remain the most engaged — and the most influential.

drillQuestions.question: Gas may power a smaller share of the world's electricity in the future, but the hours it covers will remain the most _______ — and the most expensive.

sentences.notes: 기사의 최종 문장으로, 기사 전체의 논점을 한 문장으로 응축한다. 'may'가 미래의 비중 감소를 인정하되, 'but'이 그 시간의 질적 중요성을 부각한다. 대시 뒤의 '— and the most expensive'가 마지막 강조를 더하며, 제목 'The Last Ten Percent'와 공명한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Gas may power a smaller share of the world's electricity
koreanAlignment.ko: 가스는 세계 전력의 더 작은 비중을 담당할 수 있다

koreanAlignment.en: the hours it covers will remain
koreanAlignment.ko: 가스가 담당하는 시간은 남을 것이다

koreanAlignment.en: the most consequential — and the most expensive
koreanAlignment.ko: 가장 중대한 — 그리고 가장 비싼
